verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein of the immunoglobulin superfamily with its own TM anchor. However, its surface display is tissue-restricted: it is expressed in the testis and displayed on the outer leaflet exclusively on mature spermatozoa. In non-acrosome-reacted sperm, IZUMO1 is sequestered in the acrosomal membrane (inner-facing prior to acrosome reaction). Upon the acrosome reaction, IZUMO1 redistributes to the equatorial segment of the sperm head outer plasma membrane, where it mediates gamete fusion by binding its egg-side partner JUNO (FOLR4). This cell-type-restricted (germline/gamete lineage) and activation-state-dependent (post-acrosome-reaction) outer-leaflet display with its own TM anchor maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. Outside spermatozoa, IZUMO1 is not expressed on somatic cells.
